issuer: Payment Card Industry Security Standards Council (PCI SSC)
applicability: Any entity that stores, processes, or transmits cardholder data (CHD) or sensitive authentication data (SAD)

requirements.title: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
requirements.description: Network security controls (NSCs) such as firewalls must be installed and configured to restrict inbound and outbound traffic to only what is necessary.
sub_requirements: 1.1 - Processes and mechanisms for installing and maintaining NSCs are defined and understood
1.2 - NSCs between trusted and untrusted networks are implemented
1.3 - Network access to and from the cardholder data environment is restricted
1.4 - Network connections between trusted and untrusted networks are controlled
1.5 - Risks to the CDE from computing devices able to connect to both untrusted networks and the CDE are mitigated

requirements.title: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
requirements.description: Malicious individuals often use vendor default passwords and other vendor default settings to compromise systems. Default credentials must be changed before deploying any system.
sub_requirements: 2.1 - Processes for managing all system components default security parameters are defined and understood
2.2 - System components are configured and managed securely
2.3 - Wireless environments are configured and managed securely

requirements.title: Protect Stored Account Data
requirements.description: Protection methods such as encryption, truncation, masking, and hashing are critical components of cardholder data protection.
sub_requirements: 3.1 - Processes for protecting stored account data are defined and understood
3.2 - Storage of account data is kept to a minimum
3.3 - Sensitive authentication data (SAD) is not retained after authorisation
3.4 - Access to displays of full PAN and ability to copy PAN is restricted
3.5 - Primary account number (PAN) is secured wherever it is stored
3.6 - Cryptographic keys used to protect stored account data are secured
3.7 - Where cryptography is used to protect stored account data, key management processes and procedures are defined and implemented

requirements.title: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
requirements.description: Sensitive information must be encrypted during transmission over open, public networks.
sub_requirements: 4.1 - Processes for protecting cardholder data with strong cryptography during transmission are defined
4.2 - PAN is protected with strong cryptography during transmission

requirements.title: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
requirements.description: Malicious software (malware) exploits system vulnerabilities. Anti-malware solutions must be deployed on all applicable components.
sub_requirements: 5.1 - Processes for protecting against malware are defined and understood
5.2 - Malware is prevented, or detected and addressed
5.3 - Anti-malware mechanisms and processes are active, maintained, and monitored
5.4 - Phishing and social engineering attacks are addressed

requirements.title: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
requirements.description: Unscrupulous individuals use security vulnerabilities to gain privileged access to systems. Security vulnerabilities are fixed via security patches.
sub_requirements: 6.1 - Processes for developing and maintaining secure systems and software are defined
6.2 - Bespoke and custom software are developed securely
6.3 - Security vulnerabilities are identified and addressed
6.4 - Public-facing web applications are protected against attacks
6.5 - Changes to all system components are managed securely

requirements.title: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
requirements.description: Access to system components and cardholder data should be limited to only those individuals whose job requires such access.
sub_requirements: 7.1 - Processes for restricting access to system components and cardholder data are defined and understood
7.2 - Access to system components and data is appropriately defined and assigned
7.3 - Access to system components and data is managed via an access control system

requirements.title: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
requirements.description: Two methods of authentication are required for all access to the CDE. MFA is mandatory.
sub_requirements: 8.1 - Processes for identifying and authenticating users are defined and understood
8.2 - User identification and related accounts are managed throughout the user lifecycle
8.3 - User authentication for users and administrators is established and managed
8.4 - MFA is implemented to secure access into the CDE
8.5 - MFA systems are configured to prevent misuse
8.6 - Use of application and system accounts is managed

requirements.title: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
requirements.description: Any physical access to data or systems that house cardholder data provides the opportunity for individuals to access devices or data.
sub_requirements: 9.1 - Processes and mechanisms for restricting physical access to cardholder data are defined and understood
9.2 - Physical access controls manage entry into facilities and systems containing cardholder data
9.3 - Physical access for personnel and visitors is authorised and managed
9.4 - Media with cardholder data is securely stored, accessed, distributed, and destroyed
9.5 - Point of interaction (POI) devices are protected from tampering and unauthorised substitution

requirements.title: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
requirements.description: Logging mechanisms and the ability to track user activities are critical in preventing, detecting, or minimising the impact of a data compromise.
sub_requirements: 10.1 - Processes for logging and monitoring all access to system components and cardholder data are defined
10.2 - Audit logs capture all individual user access to cardholder data
10.3 - Audit logs are protected from destruction and unauthorised modifications
10.4 - Audit logs are reviewed to identify anomalies or suspicious activity
10.5 - Audit log history is retained and available for analysis
10.6 - Time-synchronisation mechanisms support consistent time settings across all systems
10.7 - Failures of critical security controls are detected, reported, and responded to promptly

requirements.title: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
requirements.description: Vulnerabilities are continuously discovered by malicious individuals and researchers. System components, processes, and custom and bespoke software should be tested frequently.
sub_requirements: 11.1 - Processes for regularly testing security of systems and networks are defined and understood
11.2 - Wireless access points are identified and monitored; unauthorised ones are addressed
11.3 - External and internal vulnerabilities are regularly identified, prioritised, and addressed
11.4 - External and internal penetration testing is regularly performed
11.5 - Network intrusions and unexpected file changes are detected and responded to
11.6 - Unauthorised changes on payment pages are detected and responded to

requirements.title: Support Information Security with Organisational Policies and Programs
requirements.description: A strong security policy sets the security tone for the whole entity and informs personnel what is expected of them.
sub_requirements: 12.1 - A comprehensive information security policy is known and current
12.2 - Acceptable use policies for end-user technologies are defined and implemented
12.3 - Risks to the CDE are formally identified, evaluated, and managed
12.4 - PCI DSS compliance is managed
12.5 - PCI DSS scope is documented and validated
12.6 - Security awareness education is ongoing
12.7 - Personnel are screened before engagement
12.8 - Risk associated with third-party service providers (TPSPs) is managed
12.9 - Third-party service providers support customers compliance
12.10 - Suspected and confirmed security incidents are responded to immediately
